name: DURHAM LAND REGISTRY 1
units: metres (m), typed site-investigation log, transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: TOPSOIL.
annotations: shell & clay-cutter borehole (pages 1-3), 11/15.11.78

intervals.verbatim: Compact brown mottled VERY SANDY CLAY and CLAYEY fine to medium grained SAND.
Firm brown/grey mottled SILTY CLAY with occasional fine to medium sub rounded and angular gravel, and traces of sand.
Mod compact to compact brown SILTY fine to medium grained SAND.
Soft to firm grey/brown laminated SILTY CLAY, with traces of silty sand.
Compact brown SILTY fine to medium grained SAND with fine coal washings and occasional clayey sand bands.
annotations: fine coal washings (reworked coal in drift, not a seam)

intervals.verbatim: Compact brown to brown/grey SILTY fine to medium grained SAND with occasional coal washings.
annotations: occasional coal washings (reworked coal in drift, not a seam)

intervals.verbatim: Compact brown/grey becoming grey VERY SILTY fine to medium grained SAND, and SANDY SILT.
Firm to stiff grey SILTY partially laminated CLAY with occasional silt band. Texture disturbed with slickenside surfaces.
annotations: texture disturbed with slickenside surfaces (glaciotectonic/till fabric)

intervals.verbatim: Compact grey/brown slightly sandy coarse rounded and sub rounded GRAVEL and COBBLES.
Firm to stiff grey SILTY and SANDY CLAY with numerous fine to coarse sub rounded and angular gravel.
annotations: gravelly clay / till

intervals.verbatim: Very stiff grey SILTY and SANDY CLAY with numerous fine to coarse sub rounded and angular gravel, including thin band of stiff grey/brown silty laminated clay at 26.5m.
annotations: thin band of stiff grey/brown silty laminated clay at 26.5m
gravelly clay / till

intervals.verbatim: Very dense yellowish brown fine to medium grained SAND with angular sandstone fragments and traces of clay.
annotations: basal drift on weathered rockhead (angular sandstone fragments); shell & clay-cutter hole terminated at 29.40m

intervals.verbatim: Yellow/brown fine to medium grained laminated mod.weathered weak to mod.weak SANDSTONE.
annotations: rotary core borehole (pages 4-5), 14/17.12.78, air flush, cased to 27.0m; open-holed 0-29.5m over the shell hole, cored from 29.5m
first bedrock (rockhead ~29.4-29.5m)
cores especially micaceous along bedding planes (page 4 note)

intervals.verbatim: Hard friable grey finely laminated CLAYEY SHALE.
Yellow/brown fine to medium grained laminated to very thin bedded mod. to highly weathered very weak SANDSTONE. Vertically jointed with traces of clay on joint faces.
annotations: vertically jointed with traces of clay on joint faces

intervals.verbatim: Stiff grey friable SILTY CLAY.
annotations: written 'SILTY CLAY'; a thin argillaceous parting within the cored bedrock sandstone (classed mudstone, not drift)

intervals.verbatim: Yellow/brown fine to medium grained very thin bedded to thin bedded very weak to mod.weak SANDSTONE. Very weak black carbonaceous band 50mm thick at 31.15m; and local vertical joints.
modifiers: very thin bedded to thin bedded
annotations: very weak black carbonaceous band 50mm thick at 31.15m (unnamed coaly/carbonaceous band, no seam name given)
local vertical joints

intervals.verbatim: Firm grey/brown SILTY CLAY. Possibly joint infill.
annotations: written 'SILTY CLAY'; 50mm band, possibly joint infill (classed mudstone)

intervals.verbatim: Yellow/brown fine to medium grained laminated to medium bedded locally jointed slightly to mod.weathered very weak to mod.weak SANDSTONE.
annotations: locally jointed

intervals.verbatim: Grey fine grained thinly laminated to laminated friable mod.weathered very weak SHALY SILTSTONE.
annotations: shaly siltstone

intervals.verbatim: Yellow/brown laminated to medium bedded locally jointed (c.5mm clay infill) slightly to mod.weathered weak to mod.weak SANDSTONE.
annotations: locally jointed with c.5mm clay infill

intervals.verbatim: Grey thinly laminated to laminated locally friable mod.weathered to highly weathered very weak SILTSTONE with thin yellow/brown sandstone bands.
annotations: thin yellow/brown sandstone bands

intervals.verbatim: Yellow/brown becoming light grey/brown very thin to medium bedded slightly weathered weak to mod.strong SANDSTONE.
annotations: hole terminated at 40.0m; rig bogged down on wet sloping surface, awaiting arrival of bulldozer; 7 3/4 hours total

notes: COMPOSITE RECORD, two boreholes at one location, both Geological Investigations Ltd job FGE/811, Ground Level 99.25m. (1) Shell & clay-cutter borehole (pages 1-3, dated 11/15.11.78, S&CC 152mm) drilled 0-29.40m of drift -> intervals 1-13. (2) Rotary core borehole (pages 4-5, dated 14/17.12.78, Rotary 152/76mm, air flush, cased to 27.0m) open-holed 0-29.5m ('See Record of shell and claycutter borehole') then cored 29.5-40.0m of bedrock -> intervals 14-24. A 0.10m un-cored gap (29.40-29.50m) sits between the shell-hole base and the first rotary core; both agree bedrock/rockhead is at ~29.4-29.5m. Rockhead: drift over Coal Measures-type sandstone/siltstone/shale bedrock. Page-4 note: all cores especially micaceous along the bedding planes. Page 5 scan is heavily soiled/degraded in the header but the strata text and INTERFACE LEVEL column are legible and every interface level cross-checks exactly against GL 99.25m (98.95, 98.45, 97.75, 91.95, 91.45, 83.45, 80.55, 79.35, 78.25, 76.45, 71.05, 69.85, 69.75, 69.60, 69.50, 69.00, 68.85, 67.55, 67.50, 65.35, 65.05, 63.35, 62.70, 59.25) - no clerk arithmetic slips. No named coal seams and no pencil marginalia (modern 1978 typed log); the only coaly feature is the unnamed 50mm black carbonaceous band at 31.15m. Three thin argillaceous bedrock bands written as 'SILTY CLAY'/'CLAYEY SHALE' (intervals 15, 17, 19) are classed mudstone as partings within the cored sandstone, not drift. Page 6 is a 1:500 Sketch Location Plan (Appendix B Sheet No.2, DRG FGE/811/1) - no strata; not transcribed. Final depth 40.0m matches the index depth.
